meta.title: Experiences - Mouvement Research, Harvests, Bees, and Slow Days
meta.description: Farm meals, Mouvement Research, kids playground, beekeeping, olive harvest, guided tours to Dougga and Testour, stargazing, and slow days near Testour.

hero: There is always something to do. And always permission to do nothing.
items: Walk the trails. Sit under the olive trees. Watch the animals. The land is yours to explore.
Mahdi's indoor and outdoor practice space for handstands, calisthenics, rings, mobility, strength and climbing.
A family-friendly play area with trampoline-style play and open farm space for children to move, climb, run and explore.
One of the farm's best-kept secrets. The rooftop at night, far from city light.
A 90-minute session with Mahdi: movement assessment, training, and your own training plan to take home.
Suit up and visit the hives with Mahdi. Learn how the bees live and how the honey is made.
October to December - join the harvest and press your own oil. Olive oil priced separately by size and quality.
Cook and bake with the family around the open-air kitchen. Fire, food, conversation - the shared meal is included.
A professional guide takes you through Testour's Andalusian medina or the Roman city of Dougga, with an olive-oil tasting and a visit to an organic olive mill.
A farm breakfast set fresh each morning. Eggs, oil, yogurt, fruit, bread, juice.
Feed the chickens. Walk with the donkey. Spend time with the horse. The animals are daily life here.
Pomegranates, figs, almonds - depending on the season, there is always something to pick.
eventsSubhead: Time your stay around the region's festivals and Roman heritage - all within a short drive.
events: Classical theatre and music in the Roman amphitheatre at Dougga, a UNESCO World Heritage site nearby.
The town's famous autumn celebration of its pomegranate harvest, 15 minutes away.
Live jazz in the region each spring.
One of North Africa's best-preserved Roman cities - temples, theatre, and mosaics, 20-40 minutes away.
Andalusian heritage, a historic mosque, and a weekly market, 15 minutes away.
